strengths: Nearly two decades of hands-on engineering (2006–present) spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded and low-latency distributed systems, exceeding the 15-year experience bar
Recent builder-level AI experience at re:cinq: AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — directly aligned with the AI-powered product focus

Proven entrepreneurial and thought-leadership track record: founded and still runs the open-source GISCollective platform (adopted by Green Map), plus conference talks (FOSS4G) and multi-day workshops he designed and taught
concerns: Career titles top out at Staff/Senior IC, with no explicit Director-level or organization-wide strategic scope demonstrated
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and dedicated AI work is recent rather than a long-standing recognized specialty
Currently in a career gap since October 2025, so most recent AI-infrastructure work is several months in the past

key_factors.factor: Breadth and depth of engineering experience (~19 years, full stack to embedded and high-throughput systems)
Hands-on AI-agent and cloud tooling fluency (MCP generators, agentic frameworks, Kubernetes/AWS)
Founder experience and public technical evangelism (open-source platform, talks, workshops)

response.justification: The candidate comfortably clears the core bar for this role: roughly 19 years of professional engineering across an unusually wide range of domains, from embedded audio and robotics tooling to micro-services handling millions of requests per second. His most recent work building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks is exactly the kind of builder-level AI fluency the role prizes, and it sits on top of genuine cloud and infrastructure depth (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, CI/CD). Critically for a strategy-and-influence position, he has a demonstrated habit of inspiring and teaching others — founding and sustaining an open-source geo-data platform used by an international NGO, speaking at FOSS4G, and creating multi-module courses — which maps directly onto the responsibilities of advising partners and rallying teams around disruptive work. The gaps (no formal AI degree, no prior principal title) are credential gaps rather than capability gaps, and his entrepreneurial, polyglot, craftsmanship-driven profile reads as a strong practical fit for a director-level IC charged with prototyping and evangelizing AI differentiation.
